phases.name: StoryBlueprint type and Story convenience wrapper

steps.name: Create StoryBlueprint with init(json:) and unit tests
steps.description: Introduce the public StoryBlueprint type in Sources/SwiftInkRuntime/Facade/StoryBlueprint.swift. StoryBlueprint.init(json:) must run InkDecoder.probe() and InkDecoder.decode(), store the resulting ContainerNode internally, and surface the same StoryError variants that Story.init(json:) already throws. ContainerNode must remain internal; StoryBlueprint exposes no details about its parsed content. No JSONSerialization call may appear in Facade/ files (R3).
steps.criteria: Given a valid Ink JSON string with a supported inkVersion, when StoryBlueprint.init(json:) is called, then no error is thrown and a StoryBlueprint value is returned. Given a string that is not valid JSON, when StoryBlueprint.init(json:) is called, then StoryError.invalidJSON is thrown. Given an Ink JSON string with inkVersion 19, when StoryBlueprint.init(json:) is called, then StoryError.unsupportedInkVersion(19) is thrown. Given the InkDecoder probe fixture is absent or corrupt, when StoryBlueprint.init(json:) is called, then StoryError.decoderProbeFailure is thrown.

steps.name: Add Story.init(blueprint:) and refactor Story.init(json:) as convenience wrapper
steps.description: Add Story.init(blueprint: StoryBlueprint) throws as the designated initialiser on Story. It must initialise InkEngine directly from the ContainerNode held in the blueprint and must NOT call InkDecoder.probe() again. Refactor Story.init(json:) into a convenience initialiser that delegates to StoryBlueprint.init(json:) then Story.init(blueprint:). Story must remain a final class; Swift requires convenience initialisers to call a designated initialiser on the same class, so Story.init(blueprint:) becomes the single designated initialiser. Verify that a single StoryBlueprint instance can be used to create multiple Story objects and that each instance produces identical output when continued independently.
steps.criteria: Given a valid StoryBlueprint, when Story.init(blueprint:) is called twice with the same blueprint, then both Story instances independently produce the same text output when continued through the full story. Given a valid Ink JSON string, when Story.init(json:) is called, then the story behaves identically to a story created via the blueprint path (backwards compatibility preserved). When Story.init(blueprint:) is called, then JSON fixture validation occurs only once per blueprint lifecycle (no redundant validation).

steps.name: Verify all existing tests remain green after the refactor
steps.description: Run the full SwiftInkRuntimeTests suite (unit and acceptance) to confirm that the introduction of StoryBlueprint and the refactor of Story.init(json:) into a convenience wrapper have not broken any existing behaviour. All tests in WalkingSkeletonTests, Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests, Milestone2_StoryExecutionTests, and Milestone3_SaveRestoreTests must pass. No changes to those test files are permitted in this step.
steps.criteria: Given the complete SwiftInkRuntimeTests suite is executed with swift test --filter SwiftInkRuntimeTests, then every test that passed before this feature branch passes after it. Specifically: WalkingSkeletonTests story loads from a real bundle fixture and canContinue is true passes without modification. Milestone1_JSONDecodingTests Story init throws for an unsupported ink version passes without modification. Milestone2_StoryExecutionTests linear story can be continued through all text without error passes without modification. Milestone3_SaveRestoreTests (all scenarios) pass without modification.
